title: Unified Paper 60: QCD Phenomenology 4 — Lattice QCD

d_claims.claim: Lattice QCD discretises space-time on a 4D hyper-cubic lattice with spacing a. The gauge action is the Wilson plaquette action S_G = β Σ_x Σ_{μ<ν} (1 − (1/3)ℜ Tr P_{μν}(x)), where β = 6/g².
The physical observables are obtained by taking the continuum limit a → 0 while keeping the lattice volume L⁴ large enough to suppress finite-size effects.

In the FLCR framework, the lattice is the discrete–continuous bridge (Paper 8): the discrete side is the lattice gauge field U_μ(x) ∈ SU(3); the continuous side is the gauge potential A_μ(x) in the continuum limit.
The hadron masses computed in lattice QCD are consistent with experiment: m_π ≈ 140 MeV, m_ρ ≈ 770 MeV, m_N ≈ 940 MeV, m_Ω ≈ 1672 MeV.
The lattice hadron masses are the receipts (Paper 11) of the non-perturbative QCD repair. Each mass is a verifiable record of the carrier state at the confinement boundary.
In the FLCR framework, the hadron masses are the receipts (Paper 11) of the boundary repair (Paper 5): each mass is the verifiable record of the repair process that confined the quarks into hadrons.
